title: Piston cartridge for piston pump
abstract: A hydraulic piston pump (100) includes a piston (128) disposed in a blind housing bore (150) of a piston housing (124) and that reciprocates to generate a pumping action. To accommodate the piston (128), a piston cartridge (170) is installed in each of the housing bores (150). The piston cartridge (170) includes a piston sleeve (172) having an inner sleeve periphery (184), a first outer sleeve periphery (190), and a second outer sleeve periphery (192). The inner sleeve periphery (184) is configured to establish sliding contact with the piston (128). The first outer sleeve periphery (190) has a first outer sleeve diameter (194) that is larger than a second outer sleeve diameter (196) associated with the second outer sleeve periphery (192). To seal against the blind housing bore (150), a liner cap (174) having a cylindrical liner wall (210) and an axial liner cover (212) is disposed over the first outer sleeve periphery (190). The axial liner cover (212) abuts a housing bore ceiling (160) of the blind housing bore (150) and can receive hydraulic fluid there through.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A hydraulic pump (100) comprising: a piston housing (124) having a plurality of housing bores (150) disposed therein each defining a bore axis (152), each of the plurality of housing bores (150) further configured as a blind bore including a housing bore wall (158) axially terminating in a housing bore ceiling (160); a plurality of pistons (128) accommodated one each in the plurality of housing bores (150) to reciprocatingly move with respect to the bore axis (152); and a piston cartridge (170) concentrically installed in each of the plurality of housing bores (150), the piston cartridge (170) including a piston sleeve (172) having an inner sleeve periphery (184) that defines a sleeve bore (186) and is configured to allow movement of one of the plurality of pistons (128); the piston cartridge (170) further having a liner cap (174) disposed on the piston sleeve (172) and having an axial liner cover (212) extending over the sleeve bore (186) configured to establish sealing contact with the housing bore ceiling (160). 2. The hydraulic pump (100) of claim 1, wherein the axial liner cover (212) further includes fluid aperture (222) disposed therethrough that aligns with and receives hydraulic fluid from a fluid passage (162) in the housing bore ceiling (160). 3. The hydraulic pump (100) of any one of claim 1 and 2, wherein the axial liner cover (212) include an annular land (224, 226) to provide sealing contact with the housing bore ceiling (160). 4. The hydraulic pump (100) of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the annular land (224, 226)) is an inner annular land (224) disposed circumferentially proximate to the fluid aperture (222), and the axial liner cover (212) includes an outer annular land (226) disposed circumferentially about the inner annular land (224). 5. The hydraulic pump (100) of claim 1, wherein the piston sleeve (172) axially extends between a first sleeve end (180) and a second sleeve end (182), the first sleeve end (180) pressing the axial liner cover (212) adjacent the housing bore ceiling (160). 6. The hydraulic pump (100) of any one of claim 5, wherein the piston sleeve (172) including a first outer sleeve periphery (190) proximate the first sleeve end (180) and a second outer sleeve periphery (192) proximate the second sleeve end (182), the first outer sleeve periphery (190) having a first outer sleeve diameter (194) and the second outer sleeve periphery (192) having a second outer sleeve diameter (196) that is larger than the first outer sleeve diameter (194). 7. The hydraulic pump (100) of any one of claims 5 and 6, wherein the liner cap (174) includes a cylindrical liner wall (210) extending from the axial liner cover (212), the cylindrical liner wall (210) circumferentially disposed around the first outer sleeve periphery (190). 8. The hydraulic pump (100) of claim 1, wherein the piston sleeve (172) includes a plurality of radial apertures (204) disposed through the second outer sleeve periphery (192), each of the plurality of radial apertures (204) axially positioned between the first sleeve end (180) and the second sleeve end 

 (182) to communicate with a groove (166) disposed in the housing bore wall (158) of each of the plurality of the housing bores (150). 9. The hydraulic pump (100) of claim 1, wherein the piston sleeve (172) is made of a relatively harder material than the liner cap (174) such that the liner cap (174) physically deforms between the first sleeve end (180) and the housing bore ceiling (160). 10. A cartridge (170) for accommodating a piston (128) in a housing bore (160), the cartridge (100) comprising: a piston sleeve (172) extending between a first sleeve end (180) and a second sleeve end (182); the piston sleeve (172) including: an inner sleeve periphery (184) defining a sleeve bore (186) and configured to allow reciprocating movement of the piston (128); a first outer sleeve periphery (190) extending from the first sleeve end (180) and having a first outer sleeve diameter (194); and a second outer sleeve periphery (192) extending from the second sleeve end (182) and having a second outer sleeve diameter (196) than the first outer sleeve diameter (194); a liner cap (174) having a cylindrical liner wall (210) defining a liner bore (218) and radially disposed around the first outer sleeve periphery (190), the liner cap (174) further having an axial liner cover (212) extending over the liner bore (218) and having a fluid aperture (222) disposed there through to receive hydraulic fluid into the sleeve bore (186).
